notes: BG 2.23 names four media (śastra, pāvaka, āpaḥ, māruta) and denies their effect on the Self. The engineering layer preserves all four as distinct attack-modes against a contract — rewrites (cutting), fashion cycles / deprecation pressure (burning), accumulated tech debt (wetting), neglect (drying). Each map honours the elemental specificity: śastra's mode of action is decomposition by separation (cutting requires parts), and rewrites are the engineering analog (refactoring breaks the implementation into pieces). pāvaka's mode is chemical decomposition under heat, mapped to fashion-cycle-driven deprecation. āpaḥ's mode is dissolution by saturation, mapped to tech-debt accumulation. māruta's mode is desiccation by extraction, mapped to neglect. The four are preserved as distinct properties, not collapsed. Shankara's partlessness reading and Ramanuja's pervasion-by-subtlety reading are both honoured by the framing 'a contract written precisely admits no point of attack from any of these media.' PASS.
